summary.note: #370+#371 batched into 1 result, #372+#373 batched into 1 result

shortest_cycle.reason: Simple single-service JSON error test, familiar workflow by this point

longest_cycle.reason: HAR capture required manual DevTools Network tab export + separate delay test

timing_analysis.idle_gaps: Minimal — dev PC pushed new requests within 1-5 min of receiving results

repeated_failures.root_cause: SSE streaming responses require browser to process event-stream; HTTP/1.1 block response with Content-Type text/event-stream caused perpetual thinking spinner
repeated_failures.resolution: #376 switched to Content-Type application/json — BREAKTHROUGH, WARNING_VISIBLE

repeated_failures.root_cause: h2_goaway=1 kills HTTP/2 connection before warning content delivered; domain pattern mismatch in earlier tests
repeated_failures.resolution: v0 confirmed NOT_BLOCKED even with full Tier 1 settings (#369)

success_breakthrough.description: After 10 consecutive SSE failures, switching qwen3 to JSON error response (Content-Type: application/json) immediately showed WARNING_VISIBLE on first try
success_breakthrough.lesson: For SSE/streaming services, do NOT mimic the streaming format — use a simple JSON error response instead

automation_bottlenecks.description: Korean username path (최장희) and cmd shell quote/redirect issues required .cmd batch files with chcp 65001 and %USERPROFILE% expansion. Commit messages via -m flag frequently broke; workaround was write_file to C:\temp\cmNNN.txt then git commit -F
ping -n 61 always hits MCP timeout — this is EXPECTED and used as the 60s polling delay. Not a real error.
windows-mcp Snapshot labels expire between tool calls. Must re-Snapshot before every Click/Type. Forgetting this causes clicks on wrong elements.

automation_bottlenecks.issue: React state mismatch with Type tool
automation_bottlenecks.description: Direct Type tool input doesn't trigger React onChange handlers in some services. Required Clipboard set + Ctrl+V paste approach, especially for Korean text.
125% DPI scaling means physical 1920x1080 maps to logical 1536x864. SetCursorPos uses logical coords. Resolved early, no recurring issues.

workflow_efficiency.sync_delays: Minimal — git pull latency <3s, push latency <5s typically
workflow_efficiency.dev_turnaround: Dev PC typically pushed new requests within 1-5 min of test result, showing tight iteration loop
workflow_efficiency.batch_efficiency: Batching 2 requests into 1 test cycle (#370+371, #372+373) saved ~10 min each
workflow_efficiency.context_window_compaction: Session hit context limit ~3 times requiring continuation with summary. No data loss but adds ~2-3 min restart overhead each time.

suggestions_for_improvement: 1. PRE-CLASSIFY service response type (SSE/JSON/WebSocket) BEFORE writing block templates — would have saved 10 failed SSE iterations on qwen3
2. Create reusable .cmd scripts for common git operations upfront (poll, commit, push) — already done mid-session, should be standard from session start
3. Batch multiple single-service tests when possible — the 2-in-1 batches saved significant time
4. For streaming services, always try JSON error response FIRST before attempting to mimic the streaming format
5. Add a pre-flight check: before testing a warning, verify the block is actually working by checking browser DevTools Network tab for the blocked response
6. Consider longer polling intervals (120s) during known idle periods to reduce git operation overhead
7. Maintain a persistent 'lessons learned' file in the repo that carries across sessions to avoid re-discovering the same issues
overall_assessment: The #363-#377 session was highly productive despite the 10 consecutive SSE failures on qwen3. The tight dev↔test iteration loop (avg 11.4 min/cycle) enabled rapid hypothesis testing. The critical breakthrough (#376) came from abandoning the SSE approach entirely and switching to JSON — a lesson that should be applied proactively to other streaming services. Total: 15 requests processed, 13 result files delivered, 1 major breakthrough (qwen3 WARNING_VISIBLE), session duration 2h36m.
